creator: mcjgrice@gmail.com
text:
Running FLDigi4.0.4 from ports, compiled with all options.

/dev/dsp0.0 is accessible by my user account and I get a good burst of static if I use 'cat /dev/random > /dev/dsp0.0' as my normal user.

Pulseaudio and Portaudio remains unselectable in Audio options.

Trying to change the audio device crashes the application with this error:

****** Stack trace:
0x61a865 <_ZNSt3__1plIcNS_11char_traitsIcEENS_9allocatorIcEEEENS_12basic_stringIT_T0_T1_EEPKS6_RKS9_+0x1dcab5> at /usr/local/bin/fldigi
0x6625e5 <_ZNSt3__1plIcNS_11char_traitsIcEENS_9allocatorIcEEEENS_12basic_stringIT_T0_T1_EEPKS6_RKS9_+0x224835> at /usr/local/bin/fldigi
0x6623c9 <_ZNSt3__1plIcNS_11char_traitsIcEENS_9allocatorIcEEEENS_12basic_stringIT_T0_T1_EEPKS6_RKS9_+0x224619> at /usr/local/bin/fldigi
0x8026e8b55 <pthread_create+0x925> at /lib/libthr.so.3



When running fldigi with the audio device /dev/dsp0.0, hitting TX freezes the waterfall and spits multiple errors: 

E: trx_trx_transmit_loop: Sound error: Device busy. line: 352

'fstat | grep dsp' returns nothing.
